sound_type: Full clan simultaneous vocalizing -- J Pod, K Pod, and L Pod converging in greeting ceremony and vocalizing simultaneously, multi-dialect acoustic chorus, E=9
ecosystem: Greeting ceremony -- multi-pod social convergence in Salish Sea waters
season: Resident (year-round; ceremony events associated with pod reunions after separation)
milestone: SPS MAXIMUM TIED at E=9. Two E=9 events in SPS: orca breaching (degree 273, physical maximum) and orca multi-pod chorus (degree 277, social maximum). The greeting ceremony is the most emotionally charged acoustic event in Pacific Northwest marine biology. Combined E=17 with Sleater-Kinney (S36 277, E=8).

coordinates.description: Southern Resident range throughout Salish Sea. Greeting ceremonies documented particularly in San Juan Islands and Haro Strait -- primary summer foraging areas where pods most frequently converge.

ceremony_data.behavioral_sequence: Approach → lining up (pods face each other in rough lines) → charge (rush together, physical contact, rubbing) → simultaneous vocalization (all individuals, all dialects, multi-pod chorus) → extended association
ceremony_data.participants: J Pod (~23 individuals), K Pod (~17 individuals), L Pod (~35 individuals) = ~75 total Southern Resident Killer Whales
ceremony_data.frequency_range_hz: Social vocalizations approximately 1-10 kHz. Within or near human hearing range. Different from echolocation (20-100+ kHz, degree 276).
ceremony_data.social_function: Pod reunion and social bond renewal. Acoustic exchange of identity (each pod's dialect present simultaneously). Cultural behavior specific to SRKW ecotype.
ceremony_data.dialect_structure: J dialect, K dialect, L dialect -- each distinctive, learned socially, transmitted from mothers to offspring. Cross-pod familiarity: members of different pods recognize each other's dialects.
ceremony_data.e9_rationale: E=9 assigned for social and emotional intensity, not solely for acoustic source level. Physical E=9 (breach) = kinetic energy maximum. Social E=9 (chorus) = communal identity maximum. Both are genuine extremes in their respective registers.

nodes.title: SPS Social Maximum -- E=9
nodes.description: The multi-pod chorus is the most socially intense acoustic event in the SPS catalog. Two E=9 events: breach (physical) at 273, chorus (social) at 277. Both orca. The engine's two maximum events are the same species.

nodes.title: Greeting Ceremony -- Cultural Behavior
nodes.description: Behavioral sequence: approach, line up, charge, simultaneous vocalization, extended association. Specific to SRKW ecotype (transient orcas do not perform equivalent ceremony). Transmitted culturally. Social bond renewal at clan level.

nodes.title: Three Dialects Simultaneous
nodes.description: J, K, and L Pod dialects are distinctive learned call repertoires. All three present simultaneously during multi-pod chorus. Acoustic exchange of identity: each pod asserting its acoustic signature while receiving the others'.

cross_references.relationship: The other E=9 event. Physical maximum (breach) vs. social maximum (chorus). Both orca. Both E=9. Different registers of the same species' expressive range.
Orca arc closed at 276. Multi-pod chorus is post-arc, different register. Echolocation = perceptual acoustic system; chorus = social acoustic system.
Pod call = building block of the chorus. Individual call at 270 is what each individual produces during ceremony at 277. Chorus = pod call scaled to clan level.

SPS MAXIMUM TIED. Combined E=17. Rock's most important Olympia-tradition band paired with the most socially powerful orca acoustic event. Convergence in both: Brownstein + Tucker creating complete sound; J/K/L pods creating clan chorus.
